dial.summary: Net macro +2 (4 supportive: Global M2 ~$102.7T on sustained uptrend with US M2 confirmed at $23.05T in May 2026—highest ever; Fed balance sheet $6.74T holding flat per July 2026 H.4.1 data; BTC funding +0.009%/8h near-zero consistent with spot-driven accumulation; stablecoin dominance 13.1% continuing 30-day descent from mid-June's ~14.4%; 2 neutral: ISM Manufacturing 53.3% above-50-but-falling with July data due August 3, 2s10s +0.34% positive-flat as 10Y climbed to 4.71%—highest since January 2025; 2 headwinds: DXY 101.38 still above the 100 threshold after four consecutive sessions of gains, real yields 2.37% elevated and rising on 4-week basis); BTC.D firmed to 56.73% / TOTAL $2.33T recovering from July lows; July 28–29 FOMC is the primary catalyst for DXY below 100 and real yield compression, with July 30 Q2 GDP advance and June PCE as immediate post-decision macro tests; BTC Accumulation phase intact while BTC.D holds firmly above 55%.

indicators.name: Global Liquidity (Global M2)
indicators.note: 4-bank aggregate (Fed/ECB/BOJ/PBOC) at approximately $102.7T per MacroMicro series (May 2026 vintage); US M2 confirmed at $23,052.3B ($23.05T) in May 2026 per Fed H.6 release of June 23—all-time high; MacroMicro March 2026 components: US $22.67T, EU $19.4T, China $49.96T; Japan ~$9T estimated; May 2026 aggregate implied ~$102.7T; 6-8 week reporting lag means June 2026 global aggregate will not be available until mid-to-late August 2026; 4-week trend up: US M2 multi-year high, China M2 growing ~9% YoY, Mises Institute confirms broad money growth in 2026 at multi-year high including new QE; YoY growth positive and accelerating; status supportive (4-week uptrend confirmed; YoY expansion intact; contracting-YoY headwind condition not met).

indicators.name: ISM Manufacturing PMI
indicators.note: June 2026 ISM Manufacturing PMI at 53.3%, released July 1—sixth consecutive expansion month, 0.7pp below May's 54.0%; July 2026 ISM Manufacturing PMI releases Monday August 3, 2026 at 10:00 AM ET (next release—no new data today); 4-week trend down (May 54.0% → June 53.3%); sub-indicators from June: new orders 56.0% (-0.8pp), production 52.2% (-2.1pp), employment 49.7% (contracting), prices paid 73.0% (-9.1pp, largest single-month drop since July 2022); status neutral (above 50 but falling—supportive requires above 50 AND rising; headwind requires below 48 and falling; reading satisfies neither extreme condition).

indicators.name: DXY (US Dollar Index)
indicators.note: ICE DXY at 101.38 on July 24, 2026 (opened 101.44, range 101.36–101.47, current 101.381 per TradingEconomics/DealPlexus; up 0.25% in past 24 hours); DXY rising for four consecutive sessions, near top of recent range; FRED DTWEXBGS proxy-blocked; 4-week trend flat: DXY was approximately 101.0–101.1 around June 24 (post-June FOMC stabilization period) vs 101.38 today—change of +0.28%, within the <0.5% relative flat threshold; status headwind (ICE DXY at 101.38 remains above the 100 threshold; July 28–29 FOMC dovish guidance is the primary near-term catalyst for a sustained close below 100; updated from prior run's 101.12 to 101.38 reflecting four sessions of gains).

indicators.name: Real Yields (US10Y - CPI)
indicators.note: 10-year TIPS real yield (DFII10) at 2.37% as of July 2026 per MacroRadar series ('10-year real interest rate real yield is 2.37 percent, data as of July 2026, up 4.9% over the past 12 months, well above its 10-year average'); consistent with 10Y nominal at 4.71% (July 24, four-session rising streak to highest since January 2025) and implied breakeven ~2.34%; FRED API proxy-blocked; 4-week trend up: DFII10 rose approximately +0.19pp from ~2.18% on June 24 to 2.37% now, well above the 5bp flat threshold; updated +6bps from prior run's 2.31% (July 21 auction estimate vs July 24 live read); status headwind (above 2% and rising on 4-week basis; July 30 Q2 GDP advance and June PCE are the next disinflation tests).

indicators.name: 2s10s Yield Curve
indicators.note: 10-year Treasury at 4.71% (July 24, per search: 'continued to climb to 4.71% on Thursday, rising for a fourth consecutive session to its highest level since January 2025'); 2-year at 4.37% (July 21, most recent confirmed per TradingEconomics; July 22 confirmed spread: 0.36%); spread: 4.71% − 4.37% = +0.34%; updated from prior run's +0.40% (10Y rose +5bps from 4.66%, 2Y approximately flat); July 22 directly-confirmed spread was 0.36%, so current +0.34% reflects additional 10Y rise today; 4-week trend flat: spread was approximately +0.39–0.40% on June 24, now +0.34%—a ~5–6bp narrowing, at the borderline of the flat threshold and within measurement uncertainty; status neutral (positive spread well clear of the −50bps deep-inversion headwind threshold; not steepening on 30-day basis; July 28–29 FOMC dovish guidance is primary near-term catalyst for further steepening).

indicators.name: Fed Balance Sheet
indicators.note: WALCL for July 2026 at $6,735,609M = $6.74T per MacroRadar series ('Total Assets: $6,735,609 million as of July 2026, +0.4% over past 12 months, near 10-year average, -0.0% change over past 3 months'); next H.4.1 expected July 24, 2026 for week ending July 23 (may not yet be published at time of this run); FRED API proxy-blocked; 4-week trend flat: week ended ~June 25 estimated ~$6.72T; July 2026 at $6.74T—+$20B / +0.3%, within flat threshold; +0.4% YoY confirms no net QT acceleration; status supportive (flat to very slightly growing; no aggressive QT with rising overnight rates; updated from prior run's $6.72T to $6.74T).

indicators.name: BTC Funding Rates (perps avg)
indicators.note: BTC consolidating near $65K–$66K as of July 24, 2026 (per CoinReporter market analysis July 23: 'Bitcoin Consolidates Near $65K–$66K as July Rally Pauses'); CoinGlass returned HTTP 403 again this run, as did coinalyze.net and bitcoinmagazinepro.com; CF KFRI prior estimate ~0.009%/8h (annualized ~10%); near-zero funding environment consistent with BTC.D firming to 56.73% and TOTAL recovering to $2.33T; 30-day trend flat: near-zero funding persistent through June–July 2026 with no signs of speculative excess; status supportive (well below the 0.04% overheating threshold; near-zero funding during BTC.D firmness consistent with spot-driven BTC Accumulation); value carried forward from prior run due to CoinGlass 403.

indicators.name: Stablecoin Dominance
indicators.note: Stablecoin market cap $305B against total crypto $2.33T as of July 24, 2026 (per CoinGecko/crypto market search: 'Stablecoins' market cap is at $305 Billion and has a 13.1% share of the total crypto market cap'; total crypto market cap $2.33T with -0.72% 24h change); dominance: $305B / $2.33T = 13.1%; BTC dominance 56.73% (firmed from prior run's 56.3%); TOTAL recovered from $2.28T to $2.33T (+2.2% since prior run); stablecoin market cap unchanged at $305B while TOTAL rose, producing falling dominance; CoinGecko direct fetch 403; 30-day trend down: dominance was ~14.4% mid-June, 13.1% today—decline of ~130bps over 30 days; updated from prior run's 13.3% to 13.1%; status supportive (30-day downtrend intact; stablecoin dominance itself is falling, so headwind condition—'rising while TOTAL also rising'—is not met).

upcoming.title: Advance Durable Goods Orders (June 2026)
upcoming.note: Census Bureau Advance Monthly Durable Goods for June 2026 at 8:30 AM ET—core capital goods orders (ex-defense, ex-aircraft) are the key business investment sub-component; last significant growth indicator before the two-day FOMC meeting begins July 28; a solid capex print alongside soft CPI/PPI would reinforce the soft-landing case for a hold at 3.5–3.75%; weak orders could add downside risk to the growth outlook.

upcoming.title: Conference Board Consumer Confidence / FOMC Meeting Day 1 (July 28–29, 2026)
upcoming.note: Conference Board Consumer Confidence Index for July 2026 at 10:00 AM ET—1-year inflation expectations sub-component watched for anchoring signals as the FOMC convenes; two-day FOMC meeting also begins July 28; rate decision and Chair Warsh press conference on July 29 at 2:00 PM ET / 2:30 PM ET—non-SEP meeting (no dot plot); current target range: 3.5–3.75%; data the Committee will assess includes June CPI (3.5% YoY), June PPI, July Flash PMI (July 24), June durable goods (July 27); DXY at 101.38 and real yields at 2.37%—both above threshold; consensus strongly favors a hold.

upcoming.title: FOMC Rate Decision & Press Conference (July 2026)
upcoming.note: Federal Reserve rate decision at 2:00 PM ET, Chair Warsh press conference at 2:30 PM ET—non-SEP meeting (no dot plot); current target range: 3.5–3.75%; consensus favors a hold; key watch: whether Warsh acknowledges back-to-back soft CPI and PPI prints as validating the disinflationary pipeline; a dovish hold could push DXY below 100 (currently 101.38) and compress real yields toward the 2.00% neutral threshold, potentially flipping both remaining macro headwinds and strengthening the BTC Accumulation setup into Q3; September 15–16 FOMC is the next SEP meeting with updated projections; FOMC minutes from this meeting expected ~August 19, 2026.

upcoming.title: Q2 GDP Advance Estimate / Personal Income & Outlays (June PCE) / BOE Rate Decision / Jobless Claims
upcoming.note: BEA Q2 2026 GDP Advance Estimate at 8:30 AM ET—first official read on Q2 economic growth; BEA Personal Income and Outlays for June 2026 also at 8:30 AM ET—core PCE price index is the Fed's preferred monthly inflation gauge; Initial Jobless Claims (week ended July 26) also at 8:30 AM ET; Bank of England MPC decision at 12:00 BST (7:00 AM ET)—Bank Rate currently at 3.75%; July 30 BOE meeting includes publication of a new Monetary Policy Report with updated growth and inflation projections; released the morning after the July 29 FOMC decision, providing an immediate test of the soft-landing thesis.

upcoming.title: BOJ Rate Decision / Employment Cost Index Q2 / UMich Consumer Sentiment Final (July 2026)
upcoming.note: Bank of Japan monetary policy statement released overnight ET (concluding the July 30–31 BOJ meeting)—BOJ will assess whether JPY dynamics and global disinflation justify further policy normalization; Employment Cost Index Q2 2026 at 8:30 AM ET (BLS)—the Fed's preferred quarterly measure of labor cost inflation; a hot ECI would complicate the post-FOMC dovish narrative; University of Michigan Final Consumer Sentiment (July 2026) at 10:00 AM ET—final July read on 1-year and 5-year inflation expectations.
